params.name: Philosophy Meditations
params.description:
On May 19, 1641 AD, Philosophy Meditations was first published.
It is a collection of prose works created by the French philosopher René Descartes. He takes the soul and God as the two central concepts of his philosophy, and deduces the existence of God from the self, proposing the idea of "I think, therefore I am." This shifted the focus of philosophy from ontology to epistemology and made the relationship between the subject and the object of knowledge the main theme of philosophical inquir …
